input.content:
# Answer: typeflow's executor registry

## Context
Informational question — identify how typeflow maps workflow node `type` strings to their executor functions. No code changes required.

## Answer

- **File**: `server/services/executor-registry.ts`
- **Registry class**: `ExecutorRegistry` (defined at line 52; exported as the singleton `executorRegistry` at line 86)
- **Registration methods** (three, one per executor flavor):
  - `register(nodeType: string, executor: NodeExecutor)` — line 57. Standard executors. Backed by `executors: Map<string, NodeExecutor>`.
  - `registerConditional(nodeType: string, executor: ConditionalNodeExecutor)` — line 61. For branching nodes (`if`, `switch`). Backed by `conditionalExecutors: Map<string, ConditionalNodeExecutor>`.
  - `registerPassThrough(nodeType: string)` — line 65. For trigger/noop types that just forward input items. Backed by `passThroughTypes: Set<string>`.

## How the mapping works

Each registration call inserts the node-type string as a key into the corresponding `Map`/`Set`:
```ts
register(nodeType, executor) → this.executors.set(nodeType, executor)
```
Lookups go through `get(nodeType)`, `getConditional(nodeType)`, `isPassThrough(nodeType)`, or the combined `has(nodeType)` predicate.

All built-in executors are registered at module load time in the same file (lines 131–323). Examples: `executorRegistry.register("postgres", { execute: ... })`, `executorRegistry.registerConditional("if", { executeConditional: ... })`, `executorRegistry.registerPassThrough("webhook")`.

## Note on the older if/else chain
`server/services/workflow-executor.ts` still contains a long `if (currentNode.type === ...)` chain. The registry file's header comment ("Replaces the monolithic if/else chain in workflow-executor.ts with a clean registry pattern") indicates the registry is the intended replacement; whether the executor has been fully migrated to consume it is a separate question outside this task's scope.

  - `registerConditional(nodeType: string, executor: ConditionalNodeExecutor)` — line 61. For branching nodes (`if`, `switch`). Backed by `conditionalExecutors: Map<string, ConditionalNodeExecutor>`.
  - `registerPassThrough(nodeType: string)` — line 65. For trigger/noop types that just forward input items. Backed by `passThroughTypes: Set<string>`.

## How the mapping works

```ts
register(nodeType, executor) → this.executors.set(nodeType, executor)
```

Lookups go through `get(nodeType)`, `getConditional(nodeType)`, `isPassThrough(nodeType)`, or the combined `has(nodeType)` predicate.

All built-in executors are registered at module load time in the same file (lines 131–323). Examples: `executorRegistry.register("postgres", { execute: ... })`, `executorRegistry.registerConditional("if", { executeConditional: ... })`, `executorRegistry.registerPassThrough("webhook")`.

## Note on the older if/else chain
`server/services/workflow-executor.ts` still contains a long `if (currentNode.type === ...)` chain. **File**: `server/services/executor-registry.ts`
**Registry class**:

`ExecutorRegistry` (defined at line 52; exposed as the singleton `executorRegistry` exported at line 86)
**Registration methods** — three, one per fl

avor of executor:

- `register(nodeType, executor)` (line 57) → stores into
`executors: Map<string, NodeExecutor>`. Used for normal nodes (

`postgres`, `httpRequest`, `openai`, `code`, `aiAgent`, …).
- `registerCondit
ional(nodeType, executor)` (line 61) → stores into `conditionalExecutors: Map<string, ConditionalNodeExecutor>`. Used for branching nodes (`if`, `

switch`).
- `registerPassThrough(nodeType)` (line 65) → adds to `passThroughTypes: Set<string>`. Used for trigger/noop types that sim
ply forward input (`trigger`, `webhook`, `noop`, `manual

Trigger`, `scheduleTrigger`, `chatTrigger`, `workflowTrigger`).

The m
apping itself is just `Map.set(nodeType, executor)` keyed by the node
's `type` string. Lookups go through `get`, `getConditional`, `isPassThrough`, or the combined `has`
predicate. All built-in executors are wired up at module load time in the same file (lines 131–323), e.g. `executorRegistry.register("postgres
", { execute: async (node, inputItems,

ctx) => executePostgresNode(...) })`.

agging: `server/services/workflow-executor.ts` still carries a long `if (currentNode.type === ...)` chain. The registry file's header comment says

or-registry.ts` is not imported anywhere in the codebase yet — so the registry ex
ists and is fully populated, but the main `WorkflowExecutor
` does not currently consume it. If you need the *curr
ently active* dispatch mechanism (vs. the intended one),
it's still the if/else chain in `workflow-executor.ts`.

content.text:
This was a research question, not an implementation task, so I'll just answer directly.

**File**: `server/services/executor-registry.ts`
**Registry class**: `ExecutorRegistry` (defined at line 52; exposed as the singleton `executorRegistry` exported at line 86)
**Registration methods** — three, one per flavor of executor:

- `register(nodeType, executor)` (line 57) → stores into `executors: Map<string, NodeExecutor>`. Used for normal nodes (`postgres`, `httpRequest`, `openai`, `code`, `aiAgent`, …).
- `registerConditional(nodeType, executor)` (line 61) → stores into `conditionalExecutors: Map<string, ConditionalNodeExecutor>`. Used for branching nodes (`if`, `switch`).
- `registerPassThrough(nodeType)` (line 65) → adds to `passThroughTypes: Set<string>`. Used for trigger/noop types that simply forward input (`trigger`, `webhook`, `noop`, `manualTrigger`, `scheduleTrigger`, `chatTrigger`, `workflowTrigger`).

The mapping itself is just `Map.set(nodeType, executor)` keyed by the node's `type` string. Lookups go through `get`, `getConditional`, `isPassThrough`, or the combined `has` predicate. All built-in executors are wired up at module load time in the same file (lines 131–323), e.g. `executorRegistry.register("postgres", { execute: async (node, inputItems, ctx) => executePostgresNode(...) })`.

One caveat worth flagging: `server/services/workflow-executor.ts` still carries a long `if (currentNode.type === ...)` chain. The registry file's header comment says it "replaces the monolithic if/else chain in workflow-executor.ts," and `executor-registry.ts` is not imported anywhere in the codebase yet — so the registry exists and is fully populated, but the main `WorkflowExecutor` does not currently consume it. If you need the *currently active* dispatch mechanism (vs. the intended one), it's still the if/else chain in `workflow-executor.ts`.

result:
This was a research question, not an implementation task, so I'll just answer directly.

**File**: `server/services/executor-registry.ts`
**Registry class**: `ExecutorRegistry` (defined at line 52; exposed as the singleton `executorRegistry` exported at line 86)
**Registration methods** — three, one per flavor of executor:

- `register(nodeType, executor)` (line 57) → stores into `executors: Map<string, NodeExecutor>`. Used for normal nodes (`postgres`, `httpRequest`, `openai`, `code`, `aiAgent`, …).
- `registerConditional(nodeType, executor)` (line 61) → stores into `conditionalExecutors: Map<string, ConditionalNodeExecutor>`. Used for branching nodes (`if`, `switch`).
- `registerPassThrough(nodeType)` (line 65) → adds to `passThroughTypes: Set<string>`. Used for trigger/noop types that simply forward input (`trigger`, `webhook`, `noop`, `manualTrigger`, `scheduleTrigger`, `chatTrigger`, `workflowTrigger`).

The mapping itself is just `Map.set(nodeType, executor)` keyed by the node's `type` string. Lookups go through `get`, `getConditional`, `isPassThrough`, or the combined `has` predicate. All built-in executors are wired up at module load time in the same file (lines 131–323), e.g. `executorRegistry.register("postgres", { execute: async (node, inputItems, ctx) => executePostgresNode(...) })`.

One caveat worth flagging: `server/services/workflow-executor.ts` still carries a long `if (currentNode.type === ...)` chain. The registry file's header comment says it "replaces the monolithic if/else chain in workflow-executor.ts," and `executor-registry.ts` is not imported anywhere in the codebase yet — so the registry exists and is fully populated, but the main `WorkflowExecutor` does not currently consume it. If you need the *currently active* dispatch mechanism (vs. the intended one), it's still the if/else chain in `workflow-executor.ts`.
